ignored_warnings.note: FileAccess Weak — app/controllers/api/v1/assets_controller.rb:185. The original_filename is sanitised with File.basename + gsub(/[^\w.\-]/, '_') and the path is scoped under tmp/uploads/<uuid>_v1_<safe_name>. Accepted risk.

FileAccess Weak — app/controllers/api/v1/assets_controller.rb serve_local. storage_path / preview_storage_path are DB-stored values but are resolved through File.expand_path and validated against the storage/dam and tmp/ roots before use. Path traversal is not possible. Accepted risk.

SendFile Weak — app/controllers/api/v1/assets_controller.rb serve_local. Both dam_candidate and tmp_candidate (original or preview variant) are validated via File.expand_path against storage_root/tmp_root before reaching send_file. Content-Type is from DB, not user input. Accepted risk.

PermitAttributes Medium — admin/users_controller.rb:368. user_group_ids is an intentional many-to-many assignment for group membership management in the admin panel. The :admin key is handled explicitly outside user_params with role/ownership guards.

PermitAttributes High — settings_controller.rb:151. The `_id`-suffixed keys (account_id for Cloudflare R2, project_id for GCS) are storage-provider configuration strings, not ActiveRecord foreign keys. They are persisted into a Setting JSON blob (storage_config_<provider>), never mass-assigned to a model, so no privilege escalation is possible. All permitted keys mirror the storage settings form and adapter config. Accepted risk.
